2. What is you favorite episode in season 2…which one do you think will be a fan favorite(of the ones you’ve read and shot so far)?

Fast and Furious owes a lot to the Nazis.

Nitrous oxide (NAWWSSS!!!) was first used to increase the climb rate of German fighter aircraft to better intercept the high flying US and English bomber planes.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-07-15
A standard slice of pi is every flavor imaginable all at once, so it tastes a bit like a mixture of clay and Elmer's glue.  

There's one poor fellow who claimed he tried an infinitesimal slice of pi, and he said it tasted like "yellowish-indigo, with a hint of walnut."  Nobody believed him, though, and he hasn't been seen now for nearly thirty years.  There is, however, a rumor that he's safe and sound in a locked facility somewhere underneath the Nevada desert, amusing himself with his world class gyroscope collection.

### Post by takisan on 2010-09-23
If the USB port breaks off of a flash drive then is it possible to re-solder the port back on? (this is serious, no jokes please.)

---

### Post by robsoles on 2010-09-23
> **takisan said:**
> If the USB port breaks off of a flash drive then is it possible to re-solder the port back on? (this is serious, no jokes please.)

Of course it is (no joke, i am in the electronics industry amongst other things), your real problem is whether or not the violent act that broke the connector off the rest of the assembly has harmed the rest of the circuit.

With some little care, if rest of circuit is intact, it should be possible to cut the female end off a USB extension lead and connect the wires to appropriate positions for the connector on the USB stick - use a multimeter or continuity buzzer to get position of pins/wires correct.

### Post by quirkification on 2010-09-27
Hey you guys should check out nationstates
[here is mine]("http://www.nationstates.net/serene_prosperity")
It's a pretty cool site that you make your own country on and vote on legislation once or twice a day or week or on weekdays only. very limited time needed to run a country to your liking.
Be a dictatorship or a socialist country.
you can even dismiss legislation you do not want to formalize(like most governments)

Make one just for fun, it is free, check it out. PM me if you do, ill make a Ubuntu region... regions mean nothing.

Cheers,
The Quirkster

### Post by thomas144 on 2010-11-26
You have to try this! It's so weird! Go to System > Preferences > Monitors and take note of your current resolution. Then, change the resolution to the lowest it will go. If the display looks all weird or something, it will eventually (in about a minute) go back to normal. When you've had enough, change it back. My laptop's screen resolution is usually 1366 x 768, but it's now 640 x 350. I'm not sure if it's possible to mess something up by doing this, so do it at your own risk. It worked fine for me. It's so odd! I think I'll see what happens when I try to watch a You tube video.

<added>
No, don't try it! My screen resolution was so low that the Apply button in the monitors dialog didn't fit on the screen, so I had to use the Tab key to hit it. Don't make your screen resolution insanely low!

### Post by robsoles on 2010-12-19
> **takisan said:**
> You could use your own mail server. If all else, gmail is probably the best "nonpartisan in the OS world" mail server.

<offtopic>
Should I use frames in my website, or not? Opinions, Please.
</offtopic>

<on-takisan-topic>No, not even a little bit unless the desired functionality cannot be attained another way.</on-takisan-topic>

Framing someone else's page is rude and dangerous - what if they get hacked?

Framing your own page is stupid and doesn't make for a good navigation system - some of the ugliest stuff I have ever seen is a fixed menu driving one or more frames, dills end up linking externally (or even internally to a page that isn't written for their framed nav system) without using a 'target="_blank"' attribute in their anchor tag and the result is painful and stupid.

### Post by Quadunit404 on 2011-03-23
My general opinion on the people involved in the Free Software Movement is that they seriously need to get out of [s]their mom's basements[/s] the '80s. Things have changed since then, most people don't really care about modifying their software and/or operating system to work as they wish (hell even less actually change their **wallpaper** from the OS defaults) and the world isn't going to become all sugar and rainbows just because every single person in the world uses a set of cherry-picked software for the sole reason that their licenses, which are also cherry-picked, are permissive. Plus, *forcing* people to use this set of cherry-picked software licensed under also cherry-picked licenses isn't going to magically make *everybody* want to use it.

Which is why I don't participate in said movement and in some ways am opposed to it. According to the participants they're all for freedom. If making people use cherry-picked software under cherry-picked licenses and practically banning them from buying, downloading or even thinking about proprietary software or "free" software that might be at the risk of being sued over as it's a free... something for a patented... whatever is freedom, then I don't know anymore.

---

### Post by pricetech on 2011-03-24
I tried to like them, really I did.  I wanted to like both the service and the phone.  It seemed however that "they" were determined that I would loathe them in the end.

The phone, which should have been a darn good one, and probably was when it was current and where it belonged, was transformed into a piece of junk by the carrier.

The service, well let me make a distinction here;
"service" in the context of talking, texting, whatever, was acceptable since it was actually being provided by a major carrier.
"service" in the context of customer service, both personal and via website, was the most pathetic excuse I've ever encountered.

It only gets worse when I discover that I am not the exception, but rather the rule.

When will we the consumer remember that "we" outnumber "them" by a huge proportion and start acting like it.

Companies like the one that's remaining nameless to avoid any violation of the CoC need to fail miserably and in total shame.

I'm through now.

### Post by robsoles on 2011-06-19
> **catlover2 said:**
> That is odd... if a post contains just "GRR", it will come out as "Grr".

When I noticed that I decided it was 'anti-yelling' script(s) in play - if there is no lowercase in your post then it will make some of your post lowercase for you.

I was trying to post [noparse]:D[/noparse], on it's own, to make my entire reply just ':D' but it kept coming out [noparse]:d[/noparse], at the time.

I shall type my entire next post all-caps.

### Post by Quadunit404 on 2011-07-06
This was a triumph. I'm making a note here: HUGE SUCCESS. It's hard to overstate my satisfaction.

Aperture Science. We do what we must, because we can. For the good of all of us, except the ones who are dead. But there's no sense crying over every mistake. You just keep on trying 'till you run out of cake. And the science gets done, and you make a neat gun for the people who are still alive.

I'm not even angry. I'm being so sincere right now. Even though you broke my heart and killed me. And tore me to pieces. And threw every piece into a fire. As they burned it hurt because I was so happy for you! Now these points of data make a beautiful line and we're [out of beta, we're releasing on time!]("http://s3.amazonaws.com/kym-assets/photos/images/original/000/126/055/lied.gif?1306191204") So I'm GLaD. I got burned. Think of all the things we learned, for all the people who are still alive.

Go ahead and leave me. I think I prefer to stay inside. Maybe you'll find someone else to help you. [Maybe Black Mesa.]("http://s3.amazonaws.com/kym-assets/photos/images/original/000/126/055/lied.gif?1306191204") THAT WAS A JOKE, HA HA, FAT CHANCE. Anyway, this cake is great, it's so delicious and moist...

Look at me, still talking when there's science to do! When I look up there it makes me GLaD I'm **not you**. I've experiments to run, there is research to be done on the people who are still alive.

And believe me I am STILL ALIVE. I'm doing science and I'm STILL ALIVE. I feel FANTASTIC and I'm STILL ALIVE. While you're dying I'll be STILL ALIVE. And when you're dead I'll be STILL ALIVE. Still alive, s t i l l a l i v e.

### Post by PCaddicted on 2011-07-25
Hey, what mod closed the sarcasm thread???
Fortunately, it's not actually closed. I used KolourPaint to edit that screenshot.
I took a screenshot of a closed thread and copied the Thread Closed button to the picture below. Then, I used the eraser to delete the Quote, Reply with Multiple-Quote and the Quick Reply buttons.
Finally, I cut the cursor and pasted it where the 3 buttons mentioned above had been before.
So I made the screenshot look as if the thread was really closed, although it's never been locked!:P:P:P
The art of screenshot-editing is simply magnificent!

### Post by snip3r8 on 2011-09-07
I have the following piece of code
```

section .data
    hello:    db 'Hello world!',10     
    helloLen: equ $-hello        
    bye: db 'Goodbye',7
    byeLen: equ $-bye

section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov eax,4       
    mov ebx,1       
    mov ecx,hello        
    mov edx,helloLen       

    int 80h

    call procedure
    
    mov eax,1       
    mov ebx,0        

    int 80h

procedure:
    mov eax,4
    mov ebx,1
    mov ecx,bye
    mov edx,byeLen
    int 80h

```

After I call the procedure I want it  to continue the program (exiting) ,but it doesn't(i know this because it causes a segmentation fault)

[SIZE=4]Off topic or what??[/SIZE]

but seriously if you do know....

### Post by snip3r8 on 2011-09-10
[PHP]section .data
        hello:    db 'Hello world!',10     ;'hello world!' plus the linefeed character
        helloLen: equ $-hello        ;length of the hello world string

        sayTrue: db 'True',10
        sayTrueL: equ $-sayTrue

        sayFalse: db 'False',10
        sayFalseL: equ $-sayFalse

section .text
        global _start
_start:
        mov eax,4        ;The system call for write (sys_write)
        mov ebx,1        ;file descriptor 1 - standard input
        mov ecx,hello         ;put offset of hello in ecx
        mov edx,helloLen        ;[] not required because helloLen is a constant
        int 80h

        mov ebx,7
        mov ecx,8
        cmp ebx,ecx
        je  doTrue
        jne doFalse

continue:
        ;use a loop to pause a while
        a: add ecx,1
            cmp ecx,20000000000000000000000
            jle a

        mov eax,1
        mov ebx,0
        int 80h

doTrue:
        mov eax,4
        mov ebx,1
        mov ecx,sayTrue
        mov edx,sayTrueL
        int 80h
        jmp continue

doFalse:
        mov eax,4
        mov ebx,1
        mov ecx,sayFalse
        mov edx,sayFalseL
        int 80h
        jmp continue
[/PHP]

### Post by Th3Professor on 2011-09-18
> **powerpleb said:**
> Rule# &#8734;: No new rules.

"No new rules." - That's not going to happen.

"Rule# &#8734;" - That will. ;) ...in some form or another. Eventually.

> **JoshuaMiller0 said:**
> This topic is off topic. I am meant to be sayng things off topic. This means I am saying things on topic. This means I am saying things off topic!

This actually makes sense!, Read it again slowly to see!!!! ^^^^^^^
                                                                            |||||||||||

The trick isn't being off topic, which is actually on topic, so that you can be on topic in an off topic forum but, rather, to actually be off topic to the off topic forum. However, by proxy one cannot be off off topic unless they establish a recurring topic that is on topic with itself such that it functions as a subtopic. Yet then again, within the grand scheme of things, even that is... off topic.

:KS:KS:KS:KS:KS

How can I make Ubuntu run faster under Virtualbox?

---

### Post by ubupirate on 2011-10-21
> **LinuxFan999 said:**
> How can I make Ubuntu run faster under Virtualbox?

I noticed not having I/O APIC enabled greatly improved my performance with guests in vbox.

I also noticed that when I turned off virtualization technology in my BIOS (which allows me to run 64bit guest OS) and ran 32bit guests, it improved even more.

Also granting the guest more RAM seems to work too.

### Post by Quadunit404 on 2011-10-21
You can install Steam in Wine and then install TF2 through that... remember, games purchased on Steam are tied to your account rather than your computer.

However, just be aware that your graphics and performance **will** be worse than actually running the game on Windows. [This is a screenshot I took of TF2 running under Wine after the Engineer update.]("http://fc01.deviantart.net/fs71/f/2010/196/0/e/RED_is_screwed_by_Quadunit404.png") My GPU back then was low-end (nVIDIA GeForce 9100M) but even then, on Windows everything wasn't nearly as blurry. I also typically got 20 - 25FPS - **on Windows I would get 35 - 40FPS**. I am not telling you that you shouldn't run TF2 under Wine if you really want to play it again, but rather just telling you to expect worse performance than running it natively on Windows :P

btw, if you do begin playing it again, better get out your best hats and weapons because the Halloween Update is here! :wink:

### Post by takisan on 2011-11-04
People tell me it's overkill to have a boot password, a luks encryption password, and a login password. No, overkill is using gpg -s on something that's already on an encrypted disk.

In unrelated news, does anyone know how to use dd to make a blank CD? I can do it with floppy disks, but not with CD images.

command-not-found: command not found
me@desktop-oneric:~$ 

```*facepalm*

(if someone knows what this is, please tell me!)
<ontopic>
command-not-found is a bash extention that suggests packages to install if you misspell a command or type a command that doesn't exist.

Example:
```
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ xcalibb
No command 'xcalibb' found, did you mean:
 Command 'xcalib' from package 'xcalib' (universe)
xcalibb: command not found
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ 
```
```
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ nginx
The program 'nginx' can be found in the following packages:
 * nginx-extras
 * nginx-full
 * nginx-light
Try: sudo apt-get install <selected package>
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ 
```

</ontopic>

So, I was sitting in my office minding my own business when a bird flew in my window, took a crap on my keyboard, and then flew into my glass door.

---

 Command 'xcalib' from package 'xcalib' (universe)
xcalibb: command not found
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ 
``````
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ nginx
The program 'nginx' can be found in the following packages:
 * nginx-extras
 * nginx-full
 * nginx-light
Try: sudo apt-get install <selected package>
jenkinbr@jenkinbr:~/ISO$ 
```</ontopic>

### Post by Th3Professor on 2011-12-03
The other day a cat was asleep in my lap while I was at the computer writing an email.

A noise startled the cat.

The result was an instantaneous explosion of feline jetting straight up in the air and landing (of course, feet first) smack dab on top of my computer keyboard.

The email had suddenly changed languages.

** Can you type in the language of Cat (Feline)?**

**Can you translate it into English?**

So, what would this...

> lk.mu/rd6b7;oin;kgmhn,/.m gbrto;klfg...translate into in English?

:KS

### Post by IWantFroyo on 2011-12-27
Because this is the "Off Topic" game, then on topic posts are off topic and off topic posts are on topic.

Also, because the [offtopic] and [ontopic] tags are not officially recognized by the forum, they don't have to match up, as long as they're visible.

In other words I can trololol and do this:
[offtopic][offtopic][offtopic]ROFL U MAD?[/ontopic]

### Post by catlover2 on 2011-12-30
```
whatis whatis
whatis (1)           - display manual page descriptions
```Sounds good.
```
whatis whereis
whereis (1)          - locate the binary, source, and manual page files for a command
```Seems logical.
```
whatis whois
whois (1)          - client for the whois directory service
```I'll buy that.
```
whatis whyis
whyis: nothing appropriate.
```Really??!?
```
whatis howis
howis: nothing appropriate.
```How the...
```
whatis whenis                                                                                            
whenis: nothing appropriate.
```When will someone else who can code notice this??

### Post by roelforg on 2012-04-07
I like installing linux on the most exotic platforms.
Because i can!
So far:
 * Original nintendo ds
 * PS2 slim i found in the closet a few months back
 * a computer that's nothing more than a screen, kbd, mouse, mobo, psu and a usb drive
 * network gear
 * vm's with 4megs ram and no hd
 * my desktops (those are all build from parts from pc's people wanted to throw away)
 * some other stuff i can't remember
Next target:
 * recently acquired ibm ps/1 model 2121 (i'm really on the edge before using modded / modding kernels with this one)
Future dreams:
 * calculator
 * lego mindstorms nxt v1 (man, had that thing since 1 month after launch. It took way more beating than it should survive (several stairs, few glasses of soda, ...) and it still works. You can say a lot about lego, but you have to admit they build some almost indestructable stuff)
 * my parents pc, my mom freaks-out when the pc does unexpected things or somethings changed though i successfully got her to use firefox (if she only rememberd to close tabs and not be angre when ff needs ages to launch with 25 tabs on an aging winxp) nor will she use anything she don't know. short: major challenge.

### Post by roelforg on 2012-04-07
thx

and:
According to this:[http://www.adequacy.org/public/stories/2001.12.2.42056.2147.html](http://www.adequacy.org/public/stories/2001.12.2.42056.2147.html)
i (and most of you) are hackers. (i build my own sys, run linux, have some of those books, don't have those lame cursor mods or spyware, asked for isp changes and performed hw upgrades)
Which, when using it's actual meaning*, is true.
Technically, i am (besides a geek and a programmer) a white-hat cracker as i do pentests of my and my friends network (with my and his permission).

* Hacker: (1) Someone with advanced knowledge of computers (2) Someone who modifies an existing product to suit his/her needs (often used regarding computer software) -- A common mistake, partially because of hollywood and media,  is to call someone a hacker whilst actually referring to a cracker**
** Cracker: (1) bisquit (2) Someone who bypasses security measures in software (3) someone who tries to gain unauthorised access to computer systems, there are 2 kinds of these crackers: 1. white hat (they break in computer systems legally to test their security, this is done with permission of the owner/manager of said system) 2. black hat (these crackers break in to computer systems to steal data)

Q:      How many lawyers does it take to change a light bulb?
A:      Whereas the party of the first part, also known as "Lawyer", and the party of the second part, also known as "Light Bulb", do hereby and forthwith agree to a transaction wherein the party of the second part shall be removed from the current position as a result of failure to perform previously agreed upon duties, i.e., the lighting, elucidation, and otherwise illumination of the area ranging from the front (north) door, through the entryway, terminating at an area just inside the primary living area, demarcated by the beginning of the carpet, any spillover illumination being at the option of the party of the second part and not required by the aforementioned agreement between the parties.
        The aforementioned removal transaction shall include, but not be limited to, the following.  The party of the first part shall, with or without elevation at his option, by means of a chair, stepstool, ladder or any other means of elevation, grasp the party of the second part and rotate the party of the second part in a counter-clockwise direction, this point being tendered non-negotiable.  Upon reaching a point where the party of the second part becomes fully detached from the receptacle, the party of the first part shall have the option of disposing of the party of the second part in a manner consistent with all relevant and applicable local, state and federal statutes. Once separation and disposal have been achieved, the party of the first part shall have the option of beginning installation.  Aforesaid installation shall occur in a manner consistent with the reverse of the procedures described in step one of this self-same document, being careful to note that the rotation should occur in a clockwise direction, this point also being non-negotiable. The above described steps may be performed, at the option of the party of the first part, by any or all agents authorized by him, the objective being to produce the most possible revenue for the Partnership.

Be not afeard. The isle is full of noises,
Sounds and sweet airs that give delight and hurt not.
Sometimes a thousand twangling instruments
Will hum about mine ears, and sometime voices
That, if I then had waked after long sleep,
Will make me sleep again; and then, in dreaming,
The clouds methought would open, and show riches
Ready to drop upon me, that when I waked,
I cried to dream again.

> **P3rv3rs0 said:**
> Hey fellas! I have a very annoying problem. My Windows vista keeps wanting to update even right after it's been updated. I do not want to turn automatic updates off. I value my security on this issue. What can I do? Thanks!  It could be a problem of the .NET framework 4.0. To repair it in Control Panel:  Click Start > Control Panel > Programs and Features, select Microsoft .NET Framework 4 Client Profile, and click Uninstall/Change.  In the Maintenance screen that opens, choose "Repair .NET Framework 4 Client Project to its original state" and click Next. After the repair completes, click Finish. When you return to Windows Update, the .NET patches should install without a hitch.   Hope it helps. ;)

### Post by DarkAmbient on 2013-08-24
Marauder
Staining the soil, midst of stillness
Beloved fraternity to an end
Red eyes probe the scene
All the same
Stilted for the beholder
Depravity from the core
Handcarved death in stoneladen aisles

I hide the scars from my past
Yet they sense my (mute) dirge
This is when it all falls apart
White hands grasping for straws

Sly smile, poisoned glare behind
Undisguised manmade nova
Mute cry, don't dare to tread
Searing beams tracking you down

Adoring what never has been
Some will bring with them all they have seen

Searching my way to perplexion
The gleam of her eyes
In that moment she knew

Thought I could not leave this place
On this imminent day
As I've reached the final dawn
To what's gone astray
What would they care if I did stay
No-one would know
What would they care if I did stay
No-one should know

Thought I could not leave this place
On this imminent day
As I've reached the final dawn
To what's gone astray
What would they care if I did stay
No-one would know
What would they care if I did stay
No-one should know

Still brooding, soothing calm
That rigid, twisted face
Blank godhead, tear my name
Lost virtue, frantic lust

Sly smile, poisoned glare behind
Undisguised manmade nova
Mute cry, don't dare to tread
Searing beams tracking you down

Adoring what never has been
Some will bring with them all they have seen

Searching my way to perplexion
In crumbling faith I saw her
Bearing her pain in the wilderness
The gleam of her eyes
In that moment she knew

ATMs were broken into because they are running XP. **No ATMs were broken into because they had usb ports. **If they were running Windows 8 with secure boot this couldn't happen. **If they were running XP and the BIOS was setup with a password so the boot device couldn't changed this wouldn't happen.** Banks shouldn't be using Windows XP on atms anyway! **Why?
